content_en:
It seems that when Sa‘d saw Makkah in a state of surrender and helplessness, he saw flashbacks of the abuse and torture that the believers and he himself had suffered at one time at the hands of disbelievers. He recalled all the wars they had waged against the Muslims who called for monotheism just because they believed that there is no god but Allah. His stern nature made him rejoice at the Quraish's calamity and vow revenge.

On the one hand, “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, along with his companions, took the side of Abu Bakr and held fast to their opinion. On the other hand, Sa‘d Ibn *Ubaadah, along with his companions, took the other side and held fast to it. This angered many of the Prophet's Companions who held Ibn ‘Ubaadah responsible for such a dispute.

However, Sa‘d Ibn ‘Ubaadah was only being himself when he held fast to his viewpoint, for as we have already said, he always held tenaciously to his convictions and insisted on being outspoken and precise concerning exactly where he stood. This feature came out clearly at the Battle of Hunain before the Prophet himself. When the Muslims were victorious in battle, the Prophet (PBUH) always distributed the spoils of war among all the Muslims, yet on that particular day, he took special care of those whose hearts Allah had joined, namely, the elite who had committed themselves to Islam a short time before, so as to help them discipline themselves by this privilege. He did not give anything to the Muslims in whom Islam was deeply rooted, as he thought that their Islam sufficed them. He gave to the warriors who were in need. The Prophet's mere bounty was an honor most people competed for. The war booty became an important source of revenue on which the Muslims lived. Therefore, the Angaar bitterly wondered what made the Prophet deprive them of the booty .

Hassan Ibn Thaabit, the poet of the Ansaar, recited lines of poetry that mean: Go to the Prophet and say you are the best among all human beings. Why should you invite Sulaim tribe to take a share of war spoils although they are mere Muhaajiruun while you deprived the Angaar who gave shelter, support and help to Muhaajiruun. Allah called them the Ansaar because they believed in and supported the religion of guidance in the time of fierce struggle and war. They rushed to strive in the way of Allah and endured difficulties and hardships without getting weary or losing faith.

In those lines, the poet of the Prophet and the Ansaar expressed quite eloquently the embarrassment and disappointment the Angaar felt when the Prophet gave his Companions the spoils of war and did not give them anything.

The leader of the Ansaar, Sa*d Ibn *Ubaadah, realized the dilemma they were in, as he heard people talk about it secretly. This did not appeal to him, so urged by his candor, he went immediately to the Prophet (PBUH) and said, "O Messenger of Allah, this group of the Ansaar are displeased with what you did with the spoils of war. You have distributed war booty among your people and were most generous to the Arab tribes, but you did not give the Ansaar

Thus, the frank man got it off his chest and gave the Prophet a candid account of the situation. The Prophet (PBUH) asked him, “What is your opinion about it?" Sa‘d answered with the same bluntness, "I have the same viewpoint as my people." Then the Prophet (PBUH) asked him to gather the Ansaar. We must narrate the story to its very end as it is irresistably fascinating.

When the Prophet (PBUH) came where the Ansaar gathered, he looked at their disgruntled faces, and his smile brightened with gratitude and appreciation. Then he said, "O Ansaar, I heard that an incident that happened recently made you feel ill at ease. Now, didn't I find you ignorant and guided you to the way of Allah. Didn't I find you poor and Allah enriched you of His bounty? And didn't I find you enemies and Allah joined your hearts together?"

They answered, "Indeed, Allah and His Prophet are far more generous and better."

The Prophet then said, "Don't you have anything to say?”

They answered, "There is nothing to be said but that Allah and His Prophet have the grace and bounty.”

The Prophet (PBUH) then said, "By Allah, you could have justly said, We believed in you at a time when all called you a liar. We supported you at a time when you were frustrated. We gave you our money at a time when you were poor and we even sheltered you at a time when you were homeless. O Ansaar, are you upset for a thing so trivial and worldly that I gave to some people so as to join their hearts to Islam and left you out of it, believing that your Islam sufficed you? Is it not enough for you that the rest of the people will go home with a sheep or a camel, whereas you will return accompanied by the Prophet's love and appreciation? By Allah, if I were not one of the Muhaajiruun I would rather be one of the Ansgaar, and if people moved in different ways, I would choose the way taken by the Ansaar. Allah, do have mercy on the Ansaar, their children, and their children's children."

By the time the Prophet (PBUH) concluded his words, their beards were wet with tears, for the words of the great Prophet filled their hearts with tranquility and enriched their souls. All of them including Sa‘d cried out, "It is enough for us to have the Prophet's love as our reward."

In the first days of “Umar's caliphate, Sad went to the Commander of the Faithful and said with his extreme candor, "By Allah, we prefer your companion Abu Bakr over you. By Allah, I cannot stand to live near you." “Umar calmly answered, " Anyone who hates his company should seek a better one elsewhere." Sa*d said, " I will indeed seek better company somewhere else.”

Sa‘d words to ‘Umar were not an expression of hate or spite, for the man who was satisfied with the Prophet's love as his reward

cannot possibly deny loyality to such a man as ‘Umar, whom the Prophet (PBUH) had always cherished and honored. It was just that Sa*d Ibn ‘Ubaadah did not want to wait around for some event to

come up and result in an inevitable dispute between him and the Commaner of the Faithful ‘Umar Ibn AlKhattaab, a thing he did not want or accept. Sa‘d was one of the Companions whom the Qur'aan described as "merciful among themselves." He traveled to Syria. Shortly after he had settled in the Hauran plateau, he died and went back to the Lord the Most Merciful.

(47) USAAMAH IBN ZAID The Beloved Son of the Beloved

‘Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, Commander of the Faithful, sat down to distribute money from the treasury among the Muslims.

It was “Abd Allah Ibn *Umar's turn, and “Umar gave him his share. Then it was the turn of Usaamah Ibn Zaid. ‘Umar gave him double of what he gave his son “Abd Allah. As ‘Umar gave people according to their merit and endeavor, “Abd Allah Ibn ‘Umar was afraid that his position was not as highly acknowledged as he desired, to be one of the closest to Allah through his obedience, endeavor, piety, and asceticism.

Therefore, he asked his father, “You preferred Usaamah, although I experienced with the Messenger of Allah what he did not." ‘Umar answered, "Usaamah was more beloved by the Prophet (PBUH) than you were, and his father was more beloved by the Prophet (PBUH) than your father was."

Who was it, together with his father that was so close to the

Prophet's heart and love? It was a high position which Ibn “Umar did not reach, nor did his father, ‘Umar himself.

Who was it? It was Usaamah Ibn Zaid who has been called among the Prophet's Companions "The Beloved Son of the Beloved”.

His father was Zaid Ibn Haarithah*, the Prophet's servant, who preferred the Prophet (PBUH) over own his father, mother,and kin. He was with him when the Prophet (PBUH) stood in front of a large group of Companions saying, " I let you bear witness that Zaid is my son, inheriting from me and | inheriting from him."

* His biography has previously been mentioned.

His name remained Zaid Ibn Muhammad until the practice of child adoption was abolished by the Qur'aan. Usaamah is his son.

His mother was Umm Aiman, the Prophet's servant and nurse maid.

Usaamah's physical appearance made him appear a good-fornothing. Historians and narrators described him as being darkskinned and snub-nosed. By these two words, not more, did history

summarize Usaamah's physical appearance.

However, since when did Islam ever care about a person's physical characteristics? Was it not the Prophet (PBUH) who said, "Maybe a Muslim's hair is unkempt and his feet covered with dust and his clothes are not neat, but, if he swore by Allah he would fulfill his oath?"

Therefore, let us set his appearance aside. Leave his dark skin and snub nose alone; nothing of that sort has weight in Islam. Let us instead take stock of his loyalty. How was his devotion? How was his virtue? How was his honesty? How was his piety? How great was his soul? On account of the qualities of his soul, he reached a worthiness that made him eligible to receive the Prophet's infinite love and acknowledgement: " Usaamah Ibn Zaid is the most beloved to me and I wish him to be one of the virtuous. I recommend you to treat him well."

Usaamah (May Allah be pleased with him) had all the great characteristics which enabled him to be so close to the Prophet's heart and beloved in his eyes. He was the son of two generous Muslims
content_id:
Tampaknya ketika Sa'd menyaksikan Makkah yang telah menyerah dan tak berdaya, ia teringat kembali pada penyiksaan dan penderitaan yang pernah dialami oleh para mukmin termasuk dirinya sendiri di tangan kaum kafir. Ia mengenang semua perang yang mereka kobarkan melawan kaum Muslim yang menyerukan tauhid hanya karena mereka meyakini bahwa tidak ada tuhan selain Allah. Sifatnya yang keras membuatnya bergembira atas bencana yang menimpa Quraisy dan bersumpah untuk membalas dendam.

Di satu sisi, 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattab bersama para sahabatnya berpihak kepada Abu Bakr dan berpegang teguh pada pendapat mereka. Di sisi lain, Sa'd Ibn 'Ubadah bersama para pengikutnya berpegang teguh pada pendiriannya. Hal ini membuat banyak sahabat Nabi marah dan menyalahkan Ibn 'Ubadah atas perselisihan tersebut.

Namun, Sa'd Ibn 'Ubadah hanyalah menjadi dirinya sendiri ketika ia berpegang pada pandangannya, sebab seperti yang telah kami sebutkan, beliau selalu berpegang teguh pada keyakinannya dan bersikeras untuk terus terang serta tepat dalam menyatakan pendiriannya. Sifat ini tampak jelas dalam Perang Hunain di hadapan Nabi ﷺ sendiri. Ketika kaum Muslim meraih kemenangan, Nabi ﷺ selalu membagikan harta rampasan perang kepada seluruh kaum Muslim. Namun pada hari itu, beliau memberikan perhatian khusus kepada orang-orang yang hatinya baru saja Allah satukan, yaitu para tokoh yang baru masuk Islam tidak lama sebelumnya, agar mereka terdorong untuk mendalami Islam dengan keistimewaan tersebut. Beliau tidak memberikan apa pun kepada kaum Muslim yang imannya telah tertanam kuat, karena beliau berpendapat bahwa keislaman mereka sudah cukup bagi mereka. Pemberian Nabi ﷺ adalah kehormatan yang diperebutkan banyak orang. Harta rampasan perang menjadi sumber pendapatan penting bagi kaum Muslim. Maka tidak heran jika kaum Anshar merasa kecewa mengapa Nabi ﷺ tidak memberi mereka bagian dari harta rampasan.

Hassan Ibn Tsabit, penyair kaum Anshar, melantunkan bait-bait syair yang bermakna: Datanglah kepada Nabi dan katakan bahwa engkau adalah sebaik-baik manusia. Mengapa engkau mengundang suku Sulaim untuk mengambil bagian dari rampasan perang padahal mereka hanyalah kaum Muhajirin, sementara engkau tidak memberi apa pun kepada kaum Anshar yang telah memberi tempat tinggal, dukungan, dan bantuan kepada kaum Muhajirin? Allah menamai mereka Anshar karena mereka beriman kepada dan mendukung agama petunjuk di masa perjuangan dan peperangan yang sengit. Mereka berlomba berjuang di jalan Allah dan menanggung kesulitan serta penderitaan tanpa merasa lelah atau kehilangan iman.

Dalam bait-bait tersebut, penyair Nabi ﷺ dan kaum Anshar mengungkapkan dengan sangat fasih rasa malu dan kecewa yang dirasakan kaum Anshar ketika Nabi ﷺ membagi harta rampasan kepada para sahabatnya tanpa memberi mereka apa pun.

Pemimpin kaum Anshar, Sa'd Ibn 'Ubadah, menyadari dilema yang mereka hadapi ketika mendengar orang-orang membicarakannya secara diam-diam. Hal ini tidak berkenan baginya, maka didorong oleh kejujurannya, beliau segera menemui Nabi ﷺ dan berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, kelompok Anshar ini tidak puas dengan apa yang engkau lakukan terhadap harta rampasan perang. Engkau telah membagi harta rampasan kepada kaummu dan sangat dermawan kepada suku-suku Arab, namun engkau tidak memberi apa-apa kepada kaum Anshar."

Demikianlah, lelaki yang jujur itu meluapkan isi hatinya dan menyampaikan gambaran yang jujur kepada Nabi ﷺ. Nabi ﷺ bertanya kepadanya, "Apa pendapatmu tentang hal ini?" Sa'd menjawab dengan kejujuran yang sama, "Saya berpandangan sama dengan kaumku." Kemudian Nabi ﷺ memintanya untuk mengumpulkan kaum Anshar. Kita perlu menceritakan kisah ini hingga akhir karena sangat menarik dan memukau.

Ketika Nabi ﷺ tiba di tempat kaum Anshar berkumpul, beliau memandang wajah-wajah mereka yang tidak puas, dan senyumnya memancarkan rasa syukur dan penghargaan. Kemudian beliau bersabda, "Wahai kaum Anshar, aku mendengar bahwa suatu kejadian baru-baru ini membuat kalian merasa tidak nyaman. Bukankah aku mendapati kalian dalam keadaan jahil lalu Allah memberi kalian petunjuk? Bukankah aku mendapati kalian dalam keadaan miskin lalu Allah memperkaya kalian dari karunia-Nya? Dan bukankah aku mendapati kalian dalam permusuhan lalu Allah menyatukan hati kalian?"

Mereka menjawab, "Benar, Allah dan Rasul-Nya jauh lebih pemurah dan lebih baik."

Nabi ﷺ kemudian bersabda, "Tidakkah kalian mau berkata sesuatu?"

Mereka menjawab, "Tidak ada yang perlu dikatakan selain bahwa Allah dan Rasul-Nya memiliki karunia dan keutamaan."

Nabi ﷺ kemudian bersabda, "Demi Allah, kalian bisa saja mengatakan dengan adil, 'Kami beriman kepadamu di saat semua orang mendustakanmu. Kami mendukungmu di saat kamu terpuruk. Kami memberikan harta kami di saat kamu dalam kekurangan dan kami bahkan memberimu tempat tinggal di saat kamu tidak punya tempat bernaung.' Wahai kaum Anshar, apakah kalian keberatan atas hal yang begitu sepele dan duniawi yang aku berikan kepada sebagian orang untuk menarik hati mereka kepada Islam, sementara aku meninggalkan kalian karena meyakini bahwa keislaman kalian sudah cukup? Tidakkah cukup bagi kalian bahwa orang-orang lain akan pulang membawa domba atau unta, sementara kalian pulang diiringi kecintaan dan penghargaan Nabi? Demi Allah, jika aku bukan dari kaum Muhajirin, aku lebih suka menjadi bagian dari kaum Anshar, dan jika orang-orang bergerak ke arah yang berbeda, aku akan memilih jalan yang ditempuh kaum Anshar. Ya Allah, limpahkanlah rahmat kepada kaum Anshar, anak-anak mereka, dan cucu-cucu mereka."

Ketika Nabi ﷺ mengakhiri sabdanya, jenggot mereka telah basah oleh air mata, karena kata-kata sang Nabi yang agung itu memenuhi hati mereka dengan ketenteraman dan memperkaya jiwa mereka. Semua orang termasuk Sa'd berseru, "Cukuplah bagi kami kecintaan Nabi sebagai ganjaran kami."

Pada hari-hari awal kekhalifahan 'Umar, Sa'd mendatangi Amirul Mukminin dan berkata dengan kejujurannya yang luar biasa, "Demi Allah, kami lebih menyukai sahabatmu Abu Bakr daripada dirimu. Demi Allah, aku tidak tahan untuk tinggal bersamamu." 'Umar dengan tenang menjawab, "Siapa pun yang tidak suka menemaninya, biarlah ia mencari teman yang lebih baik di tempat lain." Sa'd berkata, "Sungguh aku akan mencari teman yang lebih baik di tempat lain."

Ucapan Sa'd kepada 'Umar bukanlah ungkapan kebencian atau dendam, karena orang yang puas dengan kecintaan Nabi ﷺ sebagai ganjarannya

tidak mungkin mengingkari kesetiaan kepada orang seperti 'Umar, yang selalu dimuliakan dan dihormati oleh Nabi ﷺ. Hal itu hanyalah karena Sa'd Ibn 'Ubadah tidak ingin menunggu terjadinya suatu peristiwa yang akan mengakibatkan perselisihan tak terelakkan antara dirinya dengan Amirul Mukminin 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattab — sesuatu yang tidak ia inginkan dan tidak ia terima. Sa'd adalah salah satu sahabat yang digambarkan Al-Qur'an sebagai "kasih sayang di antara sesama mereka." Beliau pun pergi ke Syam. Tidak lama setelah menetap di dataran tinggi Hauran, beliau wafat dan kembali kepada Allah Yang Maha Pengasih.

(47) USAMAH IBN ZAID — Putra Kesayangan dari Orang Kesayangan

'Umar Ibn Al-Khattab, Amirul Mukminin, duduk membagikan harta dari baitul mal kepada kaum Muslim.

Tiba giliran 'Abdullah Ibn 'Umar, dan 'Umar memberikan bagiannya. Kemudian tiba giliran Usamah Ibn Zaid. 'Umar memberikan kepadanya dua kali lipat dari yang ia berikan kepada putranya 'Abdullah. Karena 'Umar memberi sesuai dengan jasa dan pengabdian seseorang, 'Abdullah Ibn 'Umar khawatir bahwa kedudukannya tidak setinggi yang ia inginkan — yakni menjadi orang yang paling dekat kepada Allah melalui ketaatan, ketekunan, ketakwaan, dan kezuhudannya.

Maka ia bertanya kepada ayahnya, "Engkau lebih mengutamakan Usamah, padahal aku telah mengalami bersama Rasulullah apa yang tidak ia alami." 'Umar menjawab, "Usamah lebih dicintai oleh Nabi ﷺ daripada dirimu, dan ayahnya lebih dicintai oleh Nabi ﷺ daripada ayahmu."

Siapakah gerangan yang bersama ayahnya begitu dekat di hati dan dalam cinta Nabi ﷺ? Itu adalah kedudukan tinggi yang tidak dicapai oleh Ibn 'Umar, bahkan tidak pula oleh ayahnya, 'Umar sendiri.

Siapakah gerangan itu? Dialah Usamah Ibn Zaid yang dikenal di kalangan sahabat Nabi ﷺ sebagai "Putra Kesayangan dari Orang Kesayangan."

Ayahnya adalah Zaid Ibn Haritsah*, pelayan Nabi ﷺ, yang lebih memilih Nabi ﷺ daripada ayah, ibu, dan kerabatnya sendiri. Beliau bersama Nabi ﷺ ketika Nabi ﷺ berdiri di hadapan sekelompok besar sahabat dan bersabda, "Aku jadikan kalian sebagai saksi bahwa Zaid adalah putraku, mewarisi dariku dan aku mewarisi darinya."

* Biografinya telah disebutkan sebelumnya.

Namanya tetap Zaid Ibn Muhammad hingga praktik pengangkatan anak dihapuskan oleh Al-Qur'an. Usamah adalah putranya.

Ibunya adalah Ummu Aiman, pelayan dan ibu susu Nabi ﷺ.

Penampilan fisik Usamah membuatnya tampak tidak menarik. Para sejarawan dan perawi menggambarkannya berkulit gelap dan berhidung pesek. Hanya dengan dua kata itulah sejarah merangkum penampilan fisik Usamah.

Namun, sejak kapan Islam memedulikan penampilan fisik seseorang? Bukankah Nabi ﷺ yang bersabda, "Boleh jadi seorang Muslim rambutnya acak-acakan, kakinya berdebu, dan pakaiannya tidak rapi, namun jika ia bersumpah atas nama Allah, pasti ia memenuhi sumpahnya?"

Oleh karena itu, mari kita kesampingkan penampilannya. Biarkan kulitnya yang gelap dan hidungnya yang pesek; tidak ada yang seperti itu yang memiliki bobot dalam Islam. Sebaliknya, mari kita telusuri kesetiaannya. Bagaimana pengabdiannya? Bagaimana kebajikannya? Bagaimana kejujurannya? Bagaimana ketakwaannya? Seberapa agung jiwanya? Berdasarkan kualitas jiwanya, beliau mencapai kelayakan yang membuatnya berhak mendapatkan cinta dan pengakuan Nabi ﷺ yang tak terbatas: "Usamah Ibn Zaid adalah yang paling aku cintai, dan aku berharap ia menjadi salah satu orang yang saleh. Aku wasiatkan kepada kalian untuk memperlakukannya dengan baik."

Usamah radhiyallahu 'anhu memiliki semua sifat-sifat mulia yang memungkinkannya begitu dekat di hati Nabi ﷺ dan dicintai oleh beliau. Beliau adalah putra dari dua Muslim yang mulia.
content_ar:
يتناول هذا الجزء من كتاب «رجال ونساء حول الرسول» سيرة صحابي جليل أو صحابية فاضلة من الجيل الأول الذي شرفه الله بصحبة النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم وتلقّي الدين عنه مباشرةً. ويُقدّم المؤلف هذه السيرة بأسلوب حيّ يُبرز ما امتاز به هذا الصحابي من سمات الإيمان والتضحية والإخلاص.

ويُظهر كيف أن تربية النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم وصحبته أوجدت جيلاً فريداً لم يشهد التاريخ نظيره في علوّ الهمة وصدق الإيمان وكرم الأخلاق. وتُعدّ سير هؤلاء الصحابة الكرام كنزاً لا يُقدَّر للمسلمين في كل زمان ومكان.

ويختم الجزء بدروس وعبر مستفادة من هذه السيرة تُلهم المؤمن على الاقتداء بهؤلاء الصحابة الكرام والسير على منهجهم في الإخلاص لله والإيثار والتضحية في سبيل الحق.
